issue: Hello, I have completed an assessment, but my name is incorrect on the certificate. Can you please update it
subject: Certificate name update
response:
Hello! Thank you for reaching out. The good news is that you can update the name on your certificate yourself — no need to contact support for this.

Here's how to do it:

1. Open your certificate page on your HackerRank Community account.
2. In the **Full Name** field, enter the name you want to display.
3. Click **Regenerate Certificate**.
4. In the confirmation dialog, click **Update Name**.

The platform will then update your certificate with the new name.

Please note the following important points:
- You can only update your name **once per account**.
- Once changed, the name **cannot be changed again**.
- The change will apply to **all your certificates**.

If you have any trouble completing these steps, please let us know!
justification: Response is grounded in Doc 1 and Doc 3 from the Certifications FAQs (https://help.hackerrank.com/articles/8941367927-certifications-faqs), which describe the name update process, the one-time limit, and the steps to regenerate a certificate.

issue: I want Claude to stop crawling by website
subject: Website Data crawl
response:
Thank you for reaching out! To stop Anthropic's bots from crawling your website, you can add the following to your robots.txt file in your top-level directory:

User-agent: ClaudeBot
Disallow: /

Please make sure to do this for every subdomain you wish to opt out from.

Important note: Opting out requires modifying your robots.txt file as shown above. Alternate methods like blocking IP addresses may not work correctly or persistently guarantee an opt-out, as doing so could impede our ability to read your robots.txt file.

If you'd also like to limit (rather than fully block) crawling speed, you can use the Crawl-delay directive instead:

User-agent: ClaudeBot
Crawl-delay: 1

Anthropic's bots respect standard robots.txt directives, so these changes should take effect once your robots.txt file is updated.
justification: Response is based entirely on Doc 5 and Doc 1 from the article 'Does Anthropic crawl data from the web, and how can site owners block the crawler?' at https://support.claude.com/en/articles/8896518-does-anthropic-crawl-data-from-the-web-and-how-can-site-owners-block-the-crawler

issue: I need urgent cash but don't have any right now & only the VISA card
subject: Urgent need for cash
response: If you urgently need cash and only have your Visa card, you can withdraw local currency from ATMs worldwide. Look for ATMs displaying the Visa PLUS mark. You can use your Visa card (if it bears the PLUS logo) to withdraw cash using your usual PIN at these ATMs anywhere in the world. To find the nearest Visa ATM, please use the Global ATM Locator tool available at https://www.visa.com/locator/atm. If you need further assistance, please contact your card issuer or bank using the number located on the front or back of your Visa card.
justification: Response is based on Doc 1 (Visa Travel Services | Visa Travel Credit Card Support, https://www.visa.co.in/support/consumer/travel-support.html) which describes hassle-free cash withdrawal using Visa PLUS mark ATMs worldwide, and Doc 5 (same source) which references the Global ATM Locator tool at https://www.visa.com/locator/atm.

issue: i am a professor in a college and wanted to setup a claude lti key for my students
subject: Claude for students
response:
Thank you for reaching out! As a professor looking to set up Claude for your students, you'll want to use the Claude LTI integration in Canvas LMS. Here's a summary of how to get started:

1. In Canvas, sign in as an **administrator** and navigate to **Admin -> Developer Keys**.
2. Click **"+ Developer Key"** then **"+ LTI Key."**
3. Enter the following details:
   - **Key Name:** Claude LTI
   - **Description:** Enter a short description for the Canvas LTI

Please note that these steps are intended for **Claude for Education administrators and LMS administrators**. If you do not have Canvas administrator access, you may need to coordinate with your institution's LMS administrator to complete the setup.

For the full setup guide, please refer to: https://support.claude.com/en/articles/11725453-set-up-the-claude-lti-in-canvas-by-instructure

If you need further assistance or do not have the necessary admin permissions, please let us know and we can help connect you with the right resources.
justification: Response is based on Doc 1: 'Set up the Claude LTI in Canvas by Instructure' (https://support.claude.com/en/articles/11725453-set-up-the-claude-lti-in-canvas-by-instructure), which describes the steps for creating a Claude LTI Developer Key in Canvas and notes it is intended for Claude for Education and LMS administrators.

issue: i am in US Virgin Islands and the merchant is saying i have to spend minimum 10$ on my VISA card, why so?
subject: Visa card minimum spend
response:
Thank you for reaching out to Visa Support! We understand your concern about the minimum spend requirement.

In general, merchants are not permitted to establish a minimum or maximum amount for a Visa transaction. However, there is a specific exception that applies in the USA and US territories — including the US Virgin Islands, Puerto Rico, and Guam. In those locations, and only for credit cards, a merchant is permitted to require a minimum transaction amount of up to US$10.

Since you are in the US Virgin Islands, the merchant is acting within Visa's allowed policy by requiring a minimum spend of $10 on a credit card.

Please note: This exception does NOT apply to Visa debit cards. If you are using a debit card and being required to meet a minimum transaction amount, please notify your Visa card issuer, as that would not be permitted.

If you have further concerns about this merchant's behavior, you can report it by filling out the form at: https://www.visa.co.in/Forms/visa-rules.html
justification: This response is based on Doc 1 and Doc 2 from the Visa Consumer Support page (https://www.visa.co.in/support.html), which explicitly state that exceptions to the no-minimum-transaction rule apply in the USA and US territories including the US Virgin Islands, allowing merchants to require a minimum of US$10 for credit card transactions only.
